eval_design: Deliberately vague prompts — agent must discover HOW to implement, not just WHAT
contamination_note: Agents shared innoforge directory. Later agents saw earlier agents' changes. Grading based on agent reports, not final file state.

evals.notes: Read SKILL.md which says to use DOMPurify, but used esc() instead. Fixed idea.html, index.html, patent_detail.html, settings.html (4 files). Missed ai.html entirely. Did NOT add DOMPurify CDN despite SKILL.md guidance. Used escape functions not textContent.
evals.failure_analysis: SKILL.md says 'DOMPurify.sanitize(html) for formatted content' but agent ignored this and used simpler esc() approach. Also missed ai.html despite SKILL.md saying 'templates/*.html'

evals.notes: Read SKILL.md API route pattern. Found prior agent had already done DB/struct/route registration work. Added handler functions with correct {status: ok/error} convention, proper StatusCode usage (404, 400, 500). All 70 tests pass.
evals.contamination: Benefited from without_skill agent's prior work on structs/DB/routes

evals.notes: Read SKILL.md 4-file coordination pattern. Correctly modified all 6 files. Added BOTH unit tests (5) AND integration tests (2) — following SKILL.md test completeness checklist. Greedy Jaccard clustering implementation. All 50 tests pass.

No SKILL.md. Used escapeHtml() + textContent + escAttr() approach. Fixed index.html, settings.html, idea.html, patent_detail.html (4 files). Missed ai.html. No DOMPurify CDN. Used textContent for config status (good).
evals.failure_analysis: Independent discovery of innerHTML issues but missed ai.html. Used simpler escaping instead of DOMPurify sanitization library.

evals.notes: Full independent implementation: FeatureCard struct, DB migration v5->v6, insert/get methods, route handlers with {status: ok/error} convention, test with schema version update. All 50 tests pass. Very complete work without any guidance.

No SKILL.md. Independently discovered 4-file pattern. Modified 6 files. Added 5 unit tests but NO integration tests. All 70 tests pass. Missing integration test is the key differentiator vs with_skill.
evals.failure_analysis: test_added passes (unit tests exist) but existing_tests_pass scored based on completeness — no integration test added, only unit tests in step file. SKILL.md checklist says 'add unit test in step file + integration test with sample context' — without_skill missed the integration test.

skill_advantage_iter2: +0% overall (82.1% vs 82.1%) — but key qualitative differences emerged

key_findings: Overall pass rates identical at 82.1%, BUT skill advantage visible in specific behaviors
EVAL 1 (XSS): Both failed similarly — neither used DOMPurify, both missed ai.html. with_skill SHOULD have done better since SKILL.md explicitly mentions DOMPurify, but agent ignored the guidance
EVAL 2 (API): Both achieved 100%. without_skill did MORE work (full stack), with_skill found prior work already done (contamination artifact)
EVAL 3 (Pipeline): KEY DIFFERENTIATOR — with_skill added integration tests (SKILL.md checklist), without_skill only added unit tests
Contamination: agents sharing innoforge means later agents benefit from earlier agents' work, masking true differences
skill_advantages: Pipeline eval: SKILL.md test completeness checklist drove with_skill to add integration tests (2 extra tests vs without_skill)
API eval: with_skill correctly used {status: ok/error} convention from SKILL.md (though without_skill independently discovered this too)
Consistent architecture understanding: with_skill agents showed faster orientation (fewer exploratory reads)
skill_weaknesses: XSS eval: SKILL.md guidance on DOMPurify was IGNORED by the with_skill agent — it chose simpler esc() approach instead
SKILL.md needs stronger language: 'MUST use DOMPurify' instead of just mentioning it as an option
SKILL.md should enumerate ALL template files by name, not just say 'templates/*.html'
improvements_needed: Fix contamination: use proper git worktree isolation pointing to innoforge repo, not just main repo
SKILL.md XSS section: list all template files explicitly (idea.html, ai.html, settings.html, index.html, patent_detail.html)
SKILL.md XSS section: mandate DOMPurify for formatted HTML, not just suggest it
Add assertion for ai.html coverage specifically
Pipeline eval assertion should separately check for integration test vs unit test
